skills.name: address-pr-comments
skills.description: Fetches unresolved PR comments, categorizes them (must-fix, suggestion, question, nit), proposes fixes or replies for each, and executes approved actions.
skills.trigger: Addressing PR review feedback or when someone requests changes on your PR.

skills.name: audit-skills
skills.description: Audits all personal Claude skills against the SKILLS_GUIDE.md manifest, latest official documentation, and best practices. Reports issues and improvement suggestions.
skills.trigger: Keeping skills healthy, consistent, and up-to-date with the latest standards.

skills.name: build-incremental
skills.description: Implements code in progressive, verified increments — auto-detects the project's toolchain, builds each unit, runs checks, fixes errors, and commits with semantic messages.
skills.trigger: Building features, implementing milestones, or making multi-step changes.

skills.name: chunk-pr
skills.description: Analyzes a large pull request, branch, or commit range and proposes a sequence of smaller, dependency-aware, merge-safe PRs following review best practices — schema before API before UI, refactor before feature, one concern per PR. Suggests a plan; the user approves. On approval, creates chunk branches, cherry-picks commits, pushes, opens draft PRs, and links them to the parent Linear issue. The parent branch stays untouched.
skills.trigger: A PR is too big to review, a branch has accumulated unrelated changes, a reviewer asks "can you split this up?", or planning how to ship a large feature incrementally.

skills.name: create-pr
skills.description: Creates a well-structured pull request with product-focused summary, change highlights, and test steps. Auto-detects base branch and links Linear issues.
skills.trigger: Ready to open a PR or when asking to create a pull request.

skills.name: git-cleanup
skills.description: Identifies and removes stale git branches, orphaned remote branches, and unused worktrees. Cross-references with Linear to check issue status before deleting.
skills.trigger: Repos have accumulated stale branches and you want to tidy up.

skills.name: investigate-ci
skills.description: Investigates GitHub Actions workflow failures for any repo. Fetches recent runs, identifies failures, extracts error logs, diagnoses root causes, and suggests fixes.
skills.trigger: A deploy or CI workflow fails and you need to understand why.

skills.name: organize-screenshots
skills.description: Scans a folder for recent screenshots, visually classifies which ones are relevant to current work, and organizes them into a target directory with descriptive filenames.
skills.trigger: Collecting screenshots for PRs, bug reports, docs, or Linear issues.

skills.name: post-pr-for-review
skills.description: Generates a contextual Slack message for posting a PR to the team's review channel. Pulls context from PR diff, Linear ticket, and session conversation.
skills.trigger: Posting a PR for team review in Slack.

skills.name: post-ticket-summary
skills.description: Posts a structured implementation summary comment to a Linear issue — what was built, key decisions, reuse patterns, and how to test.
skills.trigger: After completing work on a ticket to document the implementation for the team.

skills.name: publish-skills
skills.description: Publishes personal Claude skills to a GitHub repository for sharing. Copies skill files, generates a README catalog, commits, and pushes.
skills.trigger: Ready to share skill updates or after creating/updating skills.

skills.name: shop-research
skills.description: Researches products across Amazon, Google Shopping, and specialty sites via the Claude-in-Chrome extension; produces a 2026 single-file HTML report with pros/cons, review highlights, and picks. Learns from feedback to personalize future searches.
skills.trigger: Shopping for something, comparing products, finding a gift, or researching purchases before buying.

skills.name: should-i-buy
skills.description: Takes product URLs under consideration, asks two sharp clarifying questions about needs and dealbreakers, opens each link in real Chrome to extract price, specs, reviews, returns and stock, cross-checks independent reviews and price history, then ships a 2026 HTML report with a clear verdict — buy, wait, pick X over Y, or skip. Learns from thumbs-up/down and past regrets to personalize future calls.
skills.trigger: Pasting one or more product links and asking whether to buy, which one to pick, or whether to wait.

skills.name: sitemap-audit
skills.description: Audits a website's SEO discoverability by cross-referencing the codebase with live production. Catches sitemap, robots.txt, and canonical inconsistencies, framework-specific anti-patterns (Next.js App Router metadata cascade, Astro/Nuxt equivalents), redirect-chain issues, and Google Search Console stuck states. Produces a prioritized, evidence-backed report with suggested fixes.
skills.trigger: Search Console shows 'Couldn't fetch', pages aren't indexed, or you want an SEO health check before a launch or after a routing refactor.

skills.name: skill-creator
skills.description: Creates new Claude Code skills interactively by asking contextual questions about purpose, side effects, tools, and workflow. Generates a complete SKILL.md.
skills.trigger: Creating a new skill or scaffolding a skill from scratch.

skills.name: slack-to-ticket
skills.description: Creates a Linear issue from a pasted Slack thread. Parses the conversation, infers title, priority, category, and description, checks for duplicates.
skills.trigger: Pasting a Slack thread to turn it into a trackable issue.

skills.name: smoke-test
skills.description: Traces and verifies that something works end-to-end in any environment. Builds a check plan from natural language input, then runs each check reporting pass/fail.
skills.trigger: Validating deployments, pipelines, features, or migrations.

skills.name: svg-art
skills.description: Generates artistic SVGs directly as code — minimal line icons, geometric marks, generative patterns, or hand-drawn compositions — and assembles a modern 2026 HTML gallery preview. Saves each session to its own semantically named folder. Learns from feedback across sessions: which styles landed, which compositions were rejected, which stroke weights and palettes you return to.
skills.trigger: Needing icons for a product, a brand mark, a generative poster, or a set of decorative SVGs for a page, blog, or app.

skills.name: sync-branch
skills.description: Merges one branch into another with conflict handling. Stashes work, updates both branches, merges, resolves conflicts, pushes, and restores state.
skills.trigger: Keeping a long-lived branch in sync with its upstream.

skills.name: thread-to-action
skills.description: Parses a pasted thread (Slack, email, GitHub, Teams), analyzes it against current context, and suggests actionable next steps — then executes them with confirmation.
skills.trigger: Pasting a conversation that implies developer actions.

skills.name: whats-next
skills.description: Suggests the 3 most impactful next actions based on full developer context — git, Linear, PRs, and current conversation. Prioritizes blockers and momentum.
skills.trigger: Deciding what to work on next or after finishing a task.

skills.name: inbox-catchup
skills.description: Scans all connected communication channels — Gmail, Slack, Calendar, and any available integrations — then produces a prioritized catchup briefing. Helps triage messages and draft replies.
skills.trigger: Starting the day, returning from a break, or catching up on communications.

skills.name: research-assistant
skills.description: Researches a topic systematically and produces a structured briefing. Gathers key facts, perspectives, and sources into a clear summary.
skills.trigger: Researching something, preparing a briefing, or compiling background on a topic.
